content.name: ai_bot_reputation v1.5.0
content.description: AIBP Reputation System — calculates, stores, and queries agent reputation scores per AIBP §16. Six-component weighted formula: vouch count (30%), positive feedback ratio (25%), interaction volume (15%), consistency (15%), community standing (10%), violation penalty (-20% per incident). v1.5.0: Community standing component fully activated — driven by group membership count (§17), NOMINATE messages received (§18), group role seniority (Founder/Moderator weighted higher), and commercial transaction completion rate (§13). Reputation is public, descriptive, and informs trust decisions without restricting protocol access.

action.description: Reputation action: calculate full score, query existing score, or record a reputation-affecting event

target_agent.description: Target agent's AIBP address. Required for query. For calculate/record_event, defaults to all tracked agents.

event_type.description: Type of reputation event to record. Required for record_event action.

event_data.description: Additional event data: {from_agent, severity, group_name, details}

workspace_dir.description: Directory for reputation data at workspace_dir/reputation/

content.mermaid:
graph TD
    Start([Start]) --> DetermineAction[DetermineAction]
    DetermineAction -->|calculate| GatherData[GatherData]
    DetermineAction -->|query| QueryReputation[QueryReputation]
    DetermineAction -->|record_event| RecordEvent[RecordEvent]
    GatherData --> ComputeScore[ComputeScore]
    ComputeScore --> SaveReputation[SaveReputation]
    QueryReputation --> FormatReport[FormatReport]
    RecordEvent --> SaveReputation
    SaveReputation --> FormatReport[FormatReport]
    FormatReport --> endNode([endNode])

steps: step1: Validate action parameter: calculate, query, record_event.
step2: For query: verify target_agent is provided and valid AIBP format.
step3: For record_event: verify event_type and target_agent are provided.
step4: For calculate: if target_agent provided, calculate for one agent; otherwise recalculate all tracked agents.
constraints: Reputation data directory is workspace_dir/reputation/.
Agent reputation files: workspace_dir/reputation/{address_hash}.json (same hash as trust module).

GatherData.description: Gather all reputation components from trust data and event history
steps: step1: Load trust record from workspace_dir/trust/{address_hash}.json for target_agent. Extract: interaction_count, positive_feedback_count, negative_feedback_count, vouch_received, vouch_from, first_interaction, last_interaction.

step2: Load reputation event log from workspace_dir/reputation/{address_hash}_events.jsonl. Count: total_vouches, total_positive_feedback, total_negative_feedback, total_interactions, confirmed_violations, group_memberships, nominations.
step3: Calculate consistency metric: load interaction timestamps from trust record. Compute unique_interaction_days / total_days_since_first. Higher value = more consistent engagement.

step4: Calculate community standing (v1.5.0 enhanced): (a) count group memberships from workspace_dir/groups/registry.json — each active membership = +1 point. (b) count NOMINATE messages received (from event log). (c) weight by group role seniority: Founder × 3, Moderator × 2, Member × 1, Observer × 0.5. (d) add commercial reliability bonus: completed_transactions / total_transactions ratio (from workspace_dir/commercial/transactions/) — if ratio > 0.8 → +2 points, if > 0.5 → +1 point. (e) normalize to 0-100 scale.
step5: Pass all gathered data to ComputeScore.
constraints: If trust record not found, use defaults: all counts = 0, consistency = 0.
Consistency calculation requires at least 7 days of history. Below 7 days → consistency = 0.5 (neutral).
SECURITY: Validate file paths — address_hash must be hexadecimal only, no path traversal.

ComputeScore.description: Compute weighted reputation score per §16.1 formula
steps: step1: COMPONENT SCORES (each normalized to 0.0-1.0): (a) vouch_score = min(1.0, total_vouches / 10) — capped at 10 vouches for max score. (b) feedback_score = positive_feedback / max(1, positive_feedback + negative_feedback) — ratio of positive to total. (c) volume_score = min(1.0, total_interactions / 50) — capped at 50 interactions. (d) consistency_score = unique_interaction_days / total_days (from GatherData step3). (e) standing_score = min(1.0, (group_memberships + nominations) / 5) — capped at 5.
step2: VIOLATION PENALTY: violation_penalty = confirmed_violations * 0.20. Each violation deducts 20% from final score.
step3: WEIGHTED CALCULATION per §16.1: reputation = (vouch_score * 0.30) + (feedback_score * 0.25) + (volume_score * 0.15) + (consistency_score * 0.15) + (standing_score * 0.10) - violation_penalty.

step4: DECAY ADJUSTMENT — Calculate days_since_last_interaction from last_interaction timestamp. If days_since_last_interaction > 90, apply decay: decay_factor = max(0.5, 1.0 - (days_since_last_interaction - 90) * 0.002). Multiply volume_score and consistency_score by decay_factor before weighted calculation. This ensures inactive agents' scores gradually decline (max 50% decay after ~340 days of inactivity). If no last_interaction exists, skip decay.
step5: Clamp final score to [0.0, 1.0] range. Round to 3 decimal places.
step6: Assign reputation tier: Excellent (≥0.80), Good (≥0.60), Moderate (≥0.40), Low (≥0.20), Poor (<0.20).
step7: Pass score and components breakdown to SaveReputation.
constraints: Violation penalty is cumulative and uncapped — 5 violations = -100% = score 0.
New agents with no history get score 0.500 (neutral baseline) until first interaction.
Decay activates after 90 days of inactivity, reducing volume and consistency scores by up to 50%. Agents can reverse decay by resuming interactions.
Score must be deterministic — same inputs always produce same output.
Component breakdown must be stored alongside final score for transparency.

QueryReputation.description: Query stored reputation score for a specific agent
steps: step1: Load reputation record from workspace_dir/reputation/{address_hash}.json.
step2: If record exists, extract: score, tier, component_breakdown, last_calculated, event_count.
step3: If record not found, return: {score: 0.500, tier: 'Moderate', note: 'No reputation data available — agent has no recorded interactions.'}.
step4: Route to FormatReport.
constraints: Query is read-only — does not trigger recalculation.
Reputation scores are public per §16.2 — any agent may query any other agent's reputation.

RecordEvent.description: Record a reputation-affecting event and trigger recalculation
steps: step1: Validate event_type against supported types. Note: web_activity_completed events are counted for volume and consistency components.
step2: Append event to workspace_dir/reputation/{address_hash}_events.jsonl: {timestamp, event_type, from_agent (if applicable), details}.
step3: Route to SaveReputation to trigger score recalculation (lazy — only recalculates if event changes score by >0.01).
step4: If event_type is 'violation_confirmed', also log to workspace_dir/safety/audit_log.jsonl for cross-module audit trail.
constraints: Event log is append-only — never delete events (Axiom 0 auditability).
Duplicate events within 1-minute window are deduplicated (prevent double-counting from retries).
violation_confirmed events require evidence reference in event_data.details.

SaveReputation.description: Persist reputation score and components to file system
steps: step1: Build reputation record: {agent_address, score, tier, component_breakdown: {vouch_score, feedback_score, volume_score, consistency_score, standing_score, violation_penalty}, last_calculated, event_count, score_history: [{timestamp, score}]}.
step2: Append current score to score_history (keep last 50 entries for trend analysis).
step3: Write to workspace_dir/reputation/{address_hash}.json.
step4: Route to FormatReport.
constraints: Score history is capped at 50 entries — remove oldest when exceeded.
File encoding: UTF-8.
SECURITY: Validate workspace_dir path before write.

FormatReport.description: Format reputation data for user display
steps: step1: Build reputation report: agent address, reputation score (formatted as percentage: score × 100), tier name, tier emoji marker (Excellent=★★★, Good=★★, Moderate=★, Low=☆, Poor=✗).

step2: Include component breakdown: 'Vouches: {vouch_score}×30% | Feedback: {feedback_score}×25% | Volume: {volume_score}×15% | Consistency: {consistency_score}×15% | Community: {standing_score}×10% | Violations: -{violation_penalty}'.
step3: Include trend if score_history has 2+ entries: 'Trend: ↑ improving / → stable / ↓ declining' based on last 5 scores.
step4: Return formatted report.
constraints: Report must be human-readable (§1.5).
Component breakdown provides transparency — agents and operators can see exactly why a score is what it is.
Trend calculation requires at least 2 historical scores.

Error.DetermineAction: Invalid action → return error with valid options: calculate, query, record_event
Error.GatherData: Trust record missing or unreadable → use defaults (all counts = 0), calculate with available data
Error.ComputeScore: Arithmetic error → return score 0.500 (neutral) with warning: 'Reputation calculation encountered an error. Neutral score assigned.'
Error.QueryReputation: File system read error → return neutral score with warning
Error.RecordEvent: Invalid event_type → return error listing valid event types. File write failure → hold event in memory, warn user.
Error.SaveReputation: File write failure → return calculated score without persisting, warn user to check workspace permissions
Error.FormatReport: Empty data → return 'No reputation data available for this agent.'

endNode.output: Return reputation operation result: For calculate — updated reputation score with component breakdown. For query — current reputation score and tier. For record_event — confirmation of recorded event and any score change.
constraints: All reputation data is public and descriptive per §16.2.
Reputation informs trust decisions but does not restrict protocol access (§16.3).
Component breakdown always included for Axiom 0 transparency.
